content: Current research treats Self-Refine as a standalone iterative self-improvement pipeline and LLM self-reflection mechanisms as core internal cognitive architectures, but they can be cross-combined into a nested two-layer reflection framework: the outer Self-Refine pipeline iteratively polishes LLM outputs, while the inner multiple-perspective self-reflection mechanisms generate higher-quality self-feedback for each outer iteration. This framework retains Self-Refine's key advantage of requiring no additional training or supervision, while addressing the common problem that generic single-perspective self-feedback often fails to spot blind spots in initial outputs.
